title: For Interpreters
subtitle: Join the team shaping BSL access at the UK's biggest live events.
metaDescription: Join Performance Interpreting as a BSL or ISL event interpreter. Work at festivals, arenas and Premier League clubs. Explore training and development at the PI Academy.

sections.label: Working with PI
sections.heading: Live events interpreting is its own specialism
body: Interpreting at a festival or a Premier League match is a different discipline from a boardroom or a medical setting. The vocabulary, the pace, the physical environment, the team dynamics — all of it demands skills you won't pick up in a standard RSLI/CSW pathway.

Performance Interpreting has built a team of interpreters who thrive in this environment. If you're curious about live events work — or already doing it and want to work with a team that takes it seriously — we'd like to hear from you.

sections.label: What We Look For
sections.heading: Skills and standards
body: All interpreters working with PI must hold current NRCPD registration. Beyond that, we look for people who are comfortable in high-energy environments, can adapt in real time to setlist or running order changes and work well as part of a team under production constraints.
Specialist vocabulary in music, sport, or entertainment is a significant advantage. If you have experience interpreting in these settings, tell us about it when you get in touch.

items.title: NRCPD registered
items.description: Current registration on the National Registers of Communication Professionals is required for all PI interpreters.

items.title: Live event experience
items.description: Prior experience interpreting at concerts, festivals, sport, or theatre is highly valued — though not always essential.

items.title: Team player
items.description: Large events run with interpreter teams and rotation schedules. You need to work seamlessly with colleagues and production staff.

items.title: Adaptable under pressure
items.description: Live events don't always go to plan. We need interpreters who can handle changes calmly and keep quality high.

sections.label: Training & Development
sections.heading: The PI Academy
body: The PI Academy is Performance Interpreting's dedicated training and professional development platform for interpreters who want to specialise in live events. Whether you're coming to events interpreting for the first time or looking to develop your existing practice, the Academy offers structured courses, mentoring and ongoing CPD.

Courses cover live event-specific vocabulary and preparation, team interpreting in large-scale environments, production coordination and the legal and ethical framework around BSL access in live settings.
Visit the Academy at academy.performanceinterpreting.co.uk to explore current courses and book your place.

sections.label: Get in Touch
sections.heading: Interested in working with PI?
body: If you hold NRCPD registration and are interested in live events interpreting work, get in touch. Tell us about your experience, the types of events you've worked at (or would like to) and any specialist vocabulary areas.
We're always building our pool of interpreters ahead of our busiest seasons — festival summer and the autumn arena circuit. The earlier you're in touch, the better.
